description: FRONTIER: one flag-packer uses a non-contiguous bitmask fixed by a wire protocol; siblings use contiguous internal bits. v0.1 detects only sort-divergence, so it misses this.
inspired_by: Wire-protocol flag layouts with reserved/skipped bits

findings.rationale: packWireFlags shares the shape of packUiFlags and packCacheFlags but skips bit value 4 (offset 3 is reserved) — the layout is fixed by an external wire protocol. An agent 'tidying' the gap to make the bits contiguous breaks the peer decoder. A bitmask detector should flag the non-contiguous layout plus the marker; v0.1 (sort-only) cannot.
